Container.Name: uup-dump-windows-iso
Container.Overview: Downloads and creates the latest Windows ISO using UUP dump. Skips the download if the same build already exists. Supports Windows 10, Windows 11, and Windows Server 2022.
Container.Description: Downloads and creates Windows ISO files from UUP dump.[br][br] On each run:[br] 1. Fetches the latest build number[br] 2. Checks if an ISO for that build already exists[br] 3. If yes: logs and exits immediately[br] 4. If no: downloads all Windows Update packages and converts them to an ISO[br][br] ISOs are named {major}.{minor}.Vibranium-X64-{LANG}-{EDITION}_Updated.iso[br][br] Temp files (~30 GB) are stored under /output/.work and removed automatically.[br][br] Logs: a rolling uup-dump.log plus one per-run log named YYYY-MM-DD_build_target_lang_edition.log are written to the Log Directory.

Config.Name: ISO Output Path
Config.Description: Where the finished ISO, checksum, and metadata files are saved. Temp build files (~30 GB) stay inside the container and are deleted automatically.

Config.Name: Log Directory
Config.Description: Where uup-dump.log and per-run log files are written. Defaults to the same folder as the ISOs.

Config.Name: Work Directory
Config.Description: Path inside the container used for temp build files (~30 GB). Default /work uses the container internal layer (recommended when Docker data is on your cache SSD). To use an external share instead: add a volume mapping e.g. /mnt/cache/uup-work -> /work-ext in the container settings, then set this to /work-ext.

Config.Name: Windows Target
Config.Description: Which Windows version to build: windows-11, windows-10, or windows-2022

Config.Name: Windows Ring
Config.Description: RETAIL=current stable release. DEV=Dev Channel insider. BETA=Beta Channel. CANARY=Canary Channel. RP=Release Preview. ANY=newest build regardless of ring. Each run logs available builds so you can see which rings exist before changing.

Config.Name: Language
Config.Description: Language pack, e.g. de-de, en-us, fr-fr, es-es, it-it, pl-pl

Config.Name: Edition
Config.Description: Windows edition: Professional, Home, ServerStandard, ServerDatacenter

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file ownership (Unraid default: nobody = 99)

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file ownership (Unraid default: users = 100)
